---
title: "Notebooks"
description: |
  Notebooks made with quarto + R code. These are personal study material: I wrote them myself to learn and practice, sometimes adapting or copy-pasting pieces from other people's work along the way. I share them here as drafts, in case they're useful to scientific collaborators or students too.
---


-   [Gaussian Processes](seewandell/GP_workbook.qmd) — I introduce Gaussian Process regression and the GP-EDM framework, working through the maths and then a few ecological toy examples.
-   [Measurement Error](measurement_error/Measurement_error_simulations.qmd) — I use `brms` to simulate how measurement error in a predictor and/or an outcome distorts a simple regression's coefficients.
-   [Priors](priors/Visualize_priors.qmd) — I visualize a handful of commonly used priors (Gamma, Exponential, Half-Cauchy, and more) so I can build some intuition about what they imply before I use them.
-   [Sample Size Planning](sample_size_planning/Sample_Size_Planning.qmd) — I simulate an ecological dataset at increasing sample sizes to show you, concretely, how the number of observations you collect shapes the power and precision of a simple linear `brms` model.
